title: lycium brevipes var. hassei
intro: Lycium brevipes var. hassei, also known as the desert box or purple box, is a type of flowering plant in the nightshade family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to have a range of benefits, including reducing stress and promoting overall well-being.
